title: Kimbell (KRP) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
author: Motley Fool Transcribing
description: Kimbell Royalty Partners reported strong Q1 2026 results with production of 25,522 BOE per day exceeding guidance midpoint. The company declared a $0.41 per unit distribution (up 11% from Q4 2025), representing an 11% annualized yield with 72% as tax-advantaged return of capital. Management affirmed full-year 2026 guidance, highlighted increased drilling activity across Bakken, Eagle Ford, and Mid-Con basins beyond the Permian, and noted active evaluation of M&A opportunities while maintaining a conservative balance sheet with 1.6x net debt to EBITDA.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong operational performance with production exceeding guidance, 11% distribution increase, improved cash generation, conservative balance sheet management, opportunistic share buybacks, and management confidence in continued growth driven by higher oil prices and robust drilling activity across diversified basin portfolio.

title: 6 Energy Stocks That Pay Us Up to 14.8% - Middle East Chaos or Not
author: Brett Owens
description: The article recommends six energy infrastructure and royalty companies offering high distribution yields (6.1%-14.8%), positioning them as reliable income sources regardless of oil price volatility. The author favors 'toll taker' pipeline companies that collect fees based on throughput rather than commodity prices, while also highlighting an MLP ETF as a tax-efficient alternative.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recommended for 6.1% distribution yield, 27 consecutive annual distribution hikes, recent record operational volumes, and active growth projects including pipeline conversions and bolt-on acquisitions.
Recommended for 7.1% distribution yield, consistent quarterly distribution increases since 2021, and strong AI boom exposure through data center and power plant connections across multiple states.

Recommended for 7.3% distribution yield, annual distribution growth every year since spinoff with double-digit improvements post-COVID, and multiple growth projects expected to come online in 2026.

Recommended for 11.3% dividend yield, direct exposure to higher oil prices with less volatility than E&P stocks, and expected marginal production growth with yields several times higher than sector average.

Recommended as preferred energy play offering nearly 8% yield with significant tax advantage through C-corp structure (1099 form instead of K-1), providing simplified tax filing compared to individual MLPs.

title: Kimbell Royalty Partners: A De-Risked 11% Yielder To Buy
author: Roberts Berzins, CFA
description: Kimbell Royalty Partners' unique royalty-based business model limits maintenance CapEx. Read why I am assigning a Buy rating for KRP stock.

title: Kimbell Royalty (KRP) Is a Great Choice for 'Trend' Investors, Here's Why
author: Zacks Equity Research
description: Kimbell Royalty (KRP) could be a great choice for investors looking to make a profit from fundamentally strong stocks that are currently on the move. It is one of the several stocks that made it through our "Recent Price Strength" screen.
